title: Crematomia huanita
taxonomy: Crematomia huanita is a species of saltwater clam, belonging to the family Ungionidae. It is classified as a type of bivalve mollusk, which is a group of marine animals characterized by their two hinged shells. The classification of Crematomia huanita is further broken down into its kingdom (Animalia), phylum (Mollusca), class (Bivalvia), order (Veneroida), family (Ungionidae), genus (Crematomia), and species (C. huanita). The exact classification of Crematomia huanita is also subject to ongoing taxonomic research and potential revisions. As with many species, the classification of Crematomia huanita serves as a fundamental framework for understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological role.
